author: Zangeti
description: Predict your next word, fix typos as you type, and suggest links to your notes. All on your device. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Zangeti
manifest.description: Predict your next word, fix typos as you type, and suggest links to your notes. All on your device.
manifest.name: Smart Autocorrect

name: Smart Autocorrect

releases.author: Zangeti
releases.description:
Housekeeping from the plugin review. No user-facing changes.

The 1.13-only settings API is now reached only where it exists, so the plugin still supports Obsidian 1.11.4 and up.

The language model files are unchanged and stay on the [1.0.0 release](https://github.com/Zangeti/obsidian-smart-autocorrect/releases/tag/1.0.0); the plugin downloads them from there on first run.
releases.name: Smart Autocorrect 1.0.3

releases.author: Zangeti
releases.description:
Adopts Obsidian's declarative settings API, so every setting is findable from settings search on 1.13.0 and later. The pane itself is unchanged, and still works the same way on older versions.

The language model files are unchanged and stay on the [1.0.0 release](https://github.com/Zangeti/obsidian-smart-autocorrect/releases/tag/1.0.0); the plugin downloads them from there on first run.
releases.name: Smart Autocorrect 1.0.2

releases.author: Zangeti
releases.description:
Fixes raised by the Obsidian plugin review. No user-facing behaviour changes.

- The plugin is no longer used as the component for markdown previews, so nothing a preview registers outlives the preview.
- Timers and element creation use `window.*` and Obsidian's `createEl` helpers, so the UI behaves in popout windows.
- The `[[` picker is docked from code rather than a stylesheet, removing a `:has()` selector and its `!important` overrides.
- LICENSE is plain MIT again (the Various Complements attribution is now in NOTICE).
- Release assets are built and attested in CI.

The language model files are unchanged and stay on the [1.0.0 release](https://github.com/Zangeti/obsidian-smart-autocorrect/releases/tag/1.0.0); the plugin downloads them from there on first run.
releases.name: Smart Autocorrect 1.0.1

releases.author: Zangeti
releases.description:
First release.

**Installing manually:** put `main.js`, `manifest.json` and `styles.css` in `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/smart-autocorrect/`, then enable the plugin. It will offer to download the language model on first run.

The three `.bin` files are the language model (86 MB total). The plugin fetches them from this release automatically, so you do not normally need to download them by hand. They are verified against these checksums:

```
be91e9d4f59786e5623bea9dbf908b1d7508b3445225d6ad03a922b01a8ae468  word_lstm.bin
803270d341771ab87eac3d382b3540b1f027a309b036b937cd6226e5c9697c69  predictive-global.bin
598418c15388fb3f67acbb59161a409b42bb4af72ef40f5c8c42440451ab1c6e  wordlist.bin
```
